title: salix × pseudosalvifolia
intro: Salix × pseudosalvifolia, a hybrid willow plant, is a cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis species, known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of this plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. Consuming Salix × pseudosalvifolia has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections.
